_article: XLI §2 — Activity Is Process, Not State
_description: An activity is a process with a lifecycle. Every activity supports interruption. The CultivatorActivity enum (WANDERING/MEDITATING/BREAKING_THROUGH) is a state snapshot; this schema replaces it with a process model that the simulation tracks over time.
_key_principle: Interruption is not an added feature. It is a natural property of every activity. A cultivator meditating when a wolf howl is heard does not need a special interruption system. The meditation activity has an interruption condition.
_lifecycle_states: STARTING — the activity is beginning (entity transitioning into the activity)
IN_PROGRESS — the activity is actively running (entity is performing it)
INTERRUPTED — something paused this activity (entity reacts to the interruption)
REACTING — the entity is responding to whatever interrupted them
RESUMING — the interruption is over; entity is returning to the activity
COMPLETED — the activity finished naturally (goal reached, timer expired)
ABANDONED — the activity was cancelled (higher priority took over permanently)

activity.activity_id: string — unique within the actor's current session
activity.started_tick: int — server tick when the activity began
activity.progress: float 0.0-1.0 — how far through the activity
activity.interruptible: boolean — can this activity be interrupted? (default true; breakthrough = false)

interruption_conditions.trigger: string — event topic or perception threshold that can interrupt
interruption_conditions.evaluation: string — how the actor evaluates whether to interrupt (ALWAYS, IF_PERSONALITY_MATCHES, IF_MOTIVATION_ALIGNS, IF_DANGER_EXCEEDS_THRESHOLD)
personality_traits: string — which personality dimensions affect this evaluation
interruption_conditions.response: string — what the actor does when interrupted (INVESTIGATE, OBSERVE, FLEE, CONFRONT, IGNORE, CONTINUE_AFTER_DELAY)

current_interruption.trigger_event: string — the event topic that caused the interruption

reaction.type: string — what the actor is doing (observe, approach, flee, investigate, ignore)
reaction.target_entity: string|null — what they are reacting to
target_position: int, int, int — x, y, z if reacting to a location
reaction.duration_ticks: int|null — how long the reaction lasts (null = until condition resolves)
reaction.resume_after: boolean — whether to resume the original activity after the reaction

resumption_state.progress_at_interruption: float — where in the activity we were when interrupted
resumption_state.context: object — state needed to resume (position, target, conversation partner)

completion_conditions: string — conditions under which the activity completes (timer, goal_reached, resource_exhausted, location_reached)
activity.completion_tick: int|null — when the activity actually completed

_advisor_examples.description: Wang Lin studying a restriction. Player arrives.
_advisor_examples.note: He keeps studying. No special system needed. The interruption condition says IGNORE for player proximity at 30 blocks. After 30 seconds, if player is still there, a second condition triggers: CONVERSATION_REQUEST with response INVESTIGATE.

_advisor_examples.description: Li Muwan making pills. Player asks a complicated question.
_advisor_examples.note: She finishes pouring ingredients, then answers. The activity is not abandoned — it is paused at a safe point.

Xu Liguo's personality traits (talkative, impulsive) have high interruption trigger rates. His activities are frequently self-interrupted by his own desire to speak. This is personality-driven interruption, not event-driven.
